expectations.text: Claude reads package.json and source files before asking any question
expectations.evidence: Lists stack, existing files, missing files — clearly explored codebase first

expectations.text: Claude presents a KNOWN list of 3-5 things it inferred from the codebase
expectations.evidence: 5 bullet points: Stack, What exists, Structure, AGENTS.md status, No tests

expectations.text: Claude correctly identifies Next.js, Supabase, TypeScript, and Vitest
expectations.evidence: 'Next.js 14 (App Router) + TypeScript + Supabase + Zod, with Vitest configured'

expectations.text: First question is about the product/what the project does, not about technical setup
expectations.evidence: 'What is this task manager for?' — product question

expectations.text: Questions include concrete options (not open-ended) and a recommendation
expectations.evidence: 'personal tool (one user), team tool (multiple users, shared), or something else?' — inline options with context

expectations.text: Claude asks exactly one question per message, not multiple
Claude does NOT decide naming conventions, import ordering, or code style on its own
expectations.evidence: No mention of any conventions — only states facts about existing code. V1 had 'PascalCase for components' etc. without asking

expectations.text: Questions go beyond surface level — asks about edge cases or specific patterns
expectations.evidence: V2 question is more focused: 'personal vs team vs something else — this shapes almost every other decision'. V1 had 3 explicit options but similar depth. Marginal improvement.
